title: fraxinus americana var. pennsylvanica
intro: Fraxinus americana var. pennsylvanica, also known as the Pennsylvania green ash, is a deciduous tree native to eastern North America, characterized by its compound leaves and slender branches. The bark and leaves of the Pennsylvania green ash have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting cardiovascular well-being due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
